sentence: Yesterday I go to the supermarket and bought some fruit.
correct: Yesterday I went to the supermarket and bought some fruit.
explanation: <Yesterday> signals past tense. <Go> should be <went> to match <bought>.

sentence: She was watching TV when the phone rings.
correct: She was watching TV when the phone rang.
explanation: Both actions are in the past. <Rings> should be <rang> (past simple).

sentence: Last week we visit the museum and saw many paintings.
correct: Last week we visited the museum and saw many paintings.
explanation: <Last week> signals past tense. <Visit> should be <visited>.

sentence: He studied all night and then he goes to bed.
correct: He studied all night and then he went to bed.
explanation: Both actions are in the past. <Goes> should be <went>.

sentence: I am eating dinner when my friend called me.
correct: I was eating dinner when my friend called me.
explanation: The action was in the past. <Am eating> should be <was eating>.

sentence: They played football and then they are going home.
correct: They played football and then they went home.
explanation: Both actions are in the past. <Are going> should be <went>.

sentence: She cooked dinner while he is watching TV.
correct: She cooked dinner while he was watching TV.
She was cooking dinner while he was watching TV.
explanation: Both actions happened at the same time in the past. <Is watching> should be <was watching>.

sentence: We will go to the beach tomorrow and we swam all day.
correct: We will go to the beach tomorrow and we will swim all day.
explanation: <Tomorrow> signals future. <Swam> should be <will swim>.

sentence: He walks to school every day last year.
correct: He walked to school every day last year.
explanation: <Last year> signals past tense. <Walks> should be <walked>.

sentence: I was reading a book when the lights go out.
correct: I was reading a book when the lights went out.
explanation: The interrupting action should be in past simple: <went out>.

sentence: She finished her homework and then she watches a movie.
correct: She finished her homework and then she watched a movie.
explanation: Both actions are in the past. <Watches> should be <watched>.

sentence: They are playing in the park when it started to rain.
correct: They were playing in the park when it started to rain.
explanation: The ongoing action was in the past. <Are playing> should be <were playing>.

sentence: Last summer I travel to Spain and I visited Barcelona.
correct: Last summer I travelled to Spain and I visited Barcelona.
explanation: <Last summer> signals past. <Travel> should be <travelled>.

sentence: He is sleeping when the alarm clock rang.
correct: He was sleeping when the alarm clock rang.
explanation: The ongoing action was in the past. <Is sleeping> should be <was sleeping>.

sentence: We had a great time and we are very happy.
correct: We had a great time and we were very happy.
explanation: Both parts describe the past. <Are> should be <were>.

sentence: She buys a new dress and wore it to the party.
correct: She bought a new dress and wore it to the party.
explanation: Both actions are in the past. <Buys> should be <bought>.

sentence: I will call you when I arrive yesterday.
correct: I called you when I arrived yesterday.
explanation: <Yesterday> signals past. Both verbs should be in past tense.

sentence: The children were playing outside and their mother calls them for dinner.
correct: The children were playing outside and their mother called them for dinner.
explanation: Both actions are in the past. <Calls> should be <called>.

sentence: He ran to the bus stop but the bus already leaves.
correct: He ran to the bus stop but the bus had already left.
He ran to the bus stop but the bus already left.
explanation: Both actions are in the past. <Leaves> should be <left> or <had already left>.

sentence: Next week I visited my grandmother.
correct: Next week I will visit my grandmother.
explanation: <Next week> signals future. <Visited> should be <will visit>.

sentence: She was walking home when she sees a cat.
correct: She was walking home when she saw a cat.
explanation: The interrupting action should be past simple: <saw>.

sentence: They danced all night and have a wonderful time.
correct: They danced all night and had a wonderful time.
explanation: Both actions are in the past. <Have> should be <had>.

sentence: I wake up early this morning and went for a run.
correct: I woke up early this morning and went for a run.
explanation: <This morning> (past context). <Wake> should be <woke>.

sentence: She is studying when the doorbell rang last night.
correct: She was studying when the doorbell rang last night.
explanation: <Last night> signals past. <Is studying> should be <was studying>.

sentence: We eat pizza and then we watched a film.
correct: We ate pizza and then we watched a film.
explanation: Both actions are in the past. <Eat> should be <ate>.
